theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Communication Svcs (+0.93%) and Materials (+0.87%) led the market today, while Health Care (-0.83%) and Industrials (-0.31%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged +0.47%, defensives +0.18%, and value/cyclicals +0.33% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.42%, Dow +0.29%, Nasdaq +0.29% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.49%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX fell 5.11% to 15.03, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold slipped 0.01% to $4,128.90, crude oil was little changed at $71.51, and bitcoin slipped 0.04% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
